seoTitle: Link a Credit Card to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to connect a business credit card to QuickBooks Online's bank feeds so transactions download and categorize automatically.
intro: Manually sorting through monthly credit card statements is a slow way to keep your books current. Linking your card directly to QuickBooks Online lets transactions flow in automatically, ready for categorizing. This guide covers finding your card issuer, authorizing the connection, and mapping it to the right liability account.

steps.heading: Open Bank Transactions
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Transactions, then Bank Transactions to reach your connected accounts screen.

steps.heading: Start a new connection
steps.body: Click the Link Account button in the upper right to begin adding your card issuer.

steps.heading: Search for your issuer
steps.body: Type your card provider's name, such as Chase, Amex, or Capital One, into the search field and select it from the verified list.

steps.heading: Log in and choose accounts
steps.body: Enter your bank credentials in the secure authorization window, then uncheck any personal cards and select only your business account.

steps.heading: Map the account and connect
steps.body: Match the card to its Credit Card liability line in your Chart of Accounts, choose your historical data range, then click Connect to pull in transactions.

faq.question: Is it safe to enter my bank login into QuickBooks?
faq.answer: The connection goes through a secure, encrypted authorization window rather than storing your credentials directly inside QuickBooks.

faq.question: How far back can I pull historical transactions?
faq.answer: You choose a historical data window during setup, which is useful for catching up on months you haven't recorded yet.

faq.question: What if my bank has both personal and business cards on one login?
faq.answer: During setup you can uncheck personal card profiles and select only the business accounts you want to sync.

faq.question: Where do the imported transactions go once connected?
faq.answer: They land in the For Review queue on your Bank Transactions screen, ready to be matched or categorized.
